title: What is Redis?
body: Redis is an in-memory data structure store used as a database, cache, message broker, and streaming engine. It supports strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets, streams, and vector embeddings.

title: Why Redis is fast
body: Redis keeps the working set in RAM and uses single-threaded event-loop I/O, avoiding lock contention. Most commands run in O(1) or O(log N) time. Typical latencies are under one millisecond.

title: Caching LLM responses
body: Calling a large language model is slow and expensive. Caching responses by an input hash turns a 800ms Gemini request into a 4ms Redis GET, often saving 90% of API spend on chatbots.

title: Semantic caching with vectors
body: Exact-match caches miss when the user rephrases. Embedding the query and searching Redis for the nearest cached vector lets paraphrases hit the same cached answer, dramatically increasing cache hit rate for LLM workloads.

title: Vector search in Redis
body: RediSearch supports HNSW and FLAT vector indexes. You store embeddings as FLOAT32 byte arrays inside hashes or JSON documents and query with KNN to retrieve the most similar items by cosine or L2 distance.

title: Retrieval Augmented Generation
body: RAG embeds a user question, retrieves the top-k most similar documents from a vector store, and stuffs them into the LLM prompt as context. Redis works as both the vector store and the response cache in one system.

title: Embeddings explained
body: An embedding model maps text into a high-dimensional vector such that semantically similar text lands close together. Gemini gemini-embedding-001 can output 768-, 1536-, or 3072-dimensional vectors suitable for cosine similarity search.

title: Rate limiting with Redis
body: Token-bucket and fixed-window rate limits are textbook Redis patterns. A single INCR plus EXPIRE per user-key gives accurate per-window counting at millions of requests per second across a fleet.

title: Distributed locks
body: SET key value NX EX seconds is the canonical Redis distributed lock primitive. Combined with a unique value-token released via Lua script, it provides safe mutual exclusion across application instances.

title: Pub Sub messaging
body: Redis PUBSUB delivers fire-and-forget messages to all subscribers of a channel. It is ideal for live UI updates, cache invalidation broadcasts, and lightweight inter-service notifications where durability is not required.

title: Redis Streams
body: Streams are append-only logs with consumer groups, message acknowledgement, and replay. They behave like a mini Kafka inside Redis, suitable for event sourcing, work queues, and audit trails.

title: Session storage
body: Web sessions stored in Redis support sliding TTL expiry and global access across stateless app servers. A HASH with EXPIRE refreshed on each request gives 30-minute idle timeouts at nanosecond cost.

title: Idempotency keys
body: When a client retries a request, an idempotency key stored in Redis with SET NX returns the prior response instead of reprocessing. This is how Stripe and AWS avoid double-charging on network flakiness.

title: Persistence
body: Redis offers two persistence modes: RDB point-in-time snapshots and AOF append-only command logs. Most production deployments use both for fast restarts and minimal data loss windows.

title: Replication and Sentinel
body: Redis replicates primary writes asynchronously to one or more replicas. Sentinel monitors the topology and promotes a replica to primary on failure within seconds, providing high availability without manual intervention.

title: Redis Cluster
body: Cluster mode shards keys across multiple primaries using 16384 hash slots. Clients query the cluster directly and follow MOVED redirects to scale write throughput linearly with node count.

title: Sorted sets for leaderboards
body: ZADD plus ZRANGE WITHSCORES makes Redis the canonical real-time leaderboard store. Reads and writes are O(log N), supporting millions of concurrent players with sub-millisecond updates.

title: RedisJSON
body: RedisJSON stores native JSON documents with path-based read, write, and atomic update. JSONPath expressions like $.user.address.city target nested fields without round-tripping the whole document.

title: Redis OM and Stack
body: Redis Stack bundles Redis with RediSearch, RedisJSON, RedisBloom, RedisTimeSeries, and RedisGraph. Redis OM provides an object-mapping client on top, treating hashes and JSON documents as typed models.

title: AI memory and chat history
body: Stateful AI agents store conversation history in Redis Lists or Streams keyed by session id. A sliding TTL plus LTRIM keeps memory bounded while preserving context across many turns of dialogue.
